evidence: our use of AI technology, tools, and services could expose us to cybersecurity, operational, intellectual property and regulatory risks
our decision to adopt AI technologies in a low-risk manner could result in slower adoption of AI technology
the Company s use of AI, including in the cybersecurity area, is managed pursuant to a corporate AI policy by a cross-functional AI Steering Committee
our use of AI technology, if not effectively governed, could result in unintended consequences, including errors, biased, and otherwise unreliable outputs
we have established AI policies and procedures
a cross-functional AI Steering Committee comprised of senior Verisign technology, cybersecurity and legal resources
the Cybersecurity Committee conducts oversight on behalf of the Board of our use of AI and AI risks, including as it pertains to cybersecurity and data governance
AI is subject to increasing regulatory scrutiny and evolving laws, rules and regulations, which may increase our compliance costs
recent advances in AI-based tools have made, and will continue to make, cyber-attacks more sophisticated, harder to defend, and easier and faster to launch

capex_mentions.quote: rapid increase in demand for compute and network hardware, including servers, chips, memory and other technology, caused by new demand from AI companies, is increasing our costs
capex_mentions.context: AI-driven hardware cost pressure on Verisign's capital and operating costs, no dollar amount specified

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-specific spend, headcount, or named vendor/cloud partnerships disclosed; Verisign describes a cautious, low-risk AI adoption posture with a named steering committee but no budget figures, warranting score 2 rather than 3.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Named governance structures (AI Steering Committee, AI policy) and named risk categories exist, but no deployed AI use cases with operational detail or metrics are described; AI is discussed primarily in the context of threats and risk management rather than internal deployment.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and highly specific; named oversight bodies (Cybersecurity Committee, AI Steering Committee), named risk categories (IP, regulatory, cybersecurity, operational), and governance mechanisms are all disclosed, supporting score 5.
